body.title: [sprint] Librarian: route its inference through the Proxy + domain router (3 bypasses)
body.description:
**Problem (found during T-inference-migrate-pinners):** the librarian device bypasses the cost-optimizing router and, worse, the Proxy itself, in THREE places: (a) `librarian/recall.py:275` calls `anthropic.Anthropic().messages.create(model='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001')` DIRECTLY; (b) `librarian/memory_writer.py:84` does the same for tagging; both violate the hard rule 'ALL inference through the Proxy — no direct OR/Anthropic calls ever'. (c) `librarian/research.py:91` dispatches `model=selection.model` where `selection` comes from librarian's own `ModelSelection` tier system (`librarian/inference.py`) — an indirect model pin that sidesteps domain routing. **Proposed shape:** (a)+(b) replace the direct `anthropic` SDK calls with `InferenceDevice().dispatch(InferenceRequest(..., task_class=<tier>, domain=''))`; (c) retire or adapt `ModelSelection` so callers pass `{task_class, domain}` and the router picks the model — no `model=` pin unless a sanctioned pin_reason applies. **Affected files:** librarian/recall.py, librarian/memory_writer.py, librarian/research.py, librarian/inference.py (ModelSelection). **Design rules:** NO SQLITE; ALL inference through the Proxy; no direct anthropic/OR imports in device code; route by {domain, task_class}; log the routing crossing. **Scope boundary:** IN: the 3 librarian bypasses -> Proxy + domain routing; retire/adapt ModelSelection's model-pinning. OUT: other devices (already migrated); the pin-gate flip. **Test plan (stub-first red->green):** (1) grep shows ZERO `import anthropic` / direct `.messages.create(` in librarian device code; (2) librarian recall/write/research dispatch InferenceRequest with a task_class + domain and no literal model pin; (3) existing librarian tests pass or are updated to assert Proxy routing. **Completion criteria:** librarian does all inference via the Proxy with domain routing; no direct anthropic calls; no unsanctioned model pins.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that every device does its inference through the Proxy and routes by {domain, task_class} — so no device holds a private model pin or a direct provider connection the cost-optimizing router can't see.
